name: Tiny Kahn
abstract: Norman "Tiny" Kahn (1923 – August 19, 1953) was an American jazz drummer, arranger, and composer. He was born in New York, United States.

about_quotes.speaker: Terry Gibbs
about_quotes.evidence: I grew up with Tiny Kahn... We were together day and night... I played that style of drums... Mel Lewis will tell you he learned drums, the feel, from Tiny Kahn... because I played that style of drums.
about_quotes.doc_title: Terry Gibbs interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, November 5, 2021 [interview 2 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Mandel Johnny
about_quotes.evidence: Tiny and I from 1945 on we all got very close. Tiny was writing arrangements... he was the most natural musician I've ever known.
about_quotes.doc_title: Mandel Johnny — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Charlie Barnet
about_quotes.evidence: Tiny Kahn took over on the drums... And Eddie Safranski was playing bass... Claude Williamson was the piano player.

about_quotes.speaker: Pete Candoli
about_quotes.evidence: Tiny Kahn, the drummer, wrote some great charts, east coast you know, and that whole group with Johnny Mandel and Al Cohn...
about_quotes.doc_title: Pete Candoli interviewed by Monk Rowe, Los Angeles, California, February 15, 1999

about_quotes.speaker: Joe Segal
about_quotes.evidence: People like Al Cohn came up, Tiny Kahn, even Sonny Rollins when he was hangin’ out here in town... Tiny Kahn was wonderful.
about_quotes.doc_title: Joe Segal — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Joe Ascione
about_quotes.evidence: And now if I say Baby Dodds, Chick Webb and George Wettling, then oh, he left out Tiny Kahn and what about Sid Catlett?
about_quotes.doc_title: Joe Ascione interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clearwater Beach, Florida, March 17, 2001
